frameworks.name: causal generative models
frameworks.one_line: human-like learners build causal models of how the world produces data

frameworks.name: intuitive physics and psychology
frameworks.one_line: learning is grounded in early-developing core theories of objects and agents

frameworks.name: compositionality and learning-to-learn
frameworks.one_line: structured reuse of primitives enables rapid generalization to new tasks

frameworks.name: deep neural networks (end-to-end)
frameworks.one_line: pattern-recognition systems that achieve human-level task scores without these ingredients

core_question: What core ingredients beyond current deep-learning practice are needed for machines to learn and think like people?
key_assumptions: human-like intelligence is qualitatively distinct from end-to-end pattern recognition
cognitive science offers prescriptive constraints, not just inspiration, for AI design
causal, compositional structure is central to human learning and generalization
manipulations: contrast human cognitive competencies against capabilities of deep networks in recognition, video games, and board games
key_findings: deep networks match or exceed human performance on several benchmark tasks
those systems nonetheless diverge from human intelligence in crucial ways (data efficiency, generalization, causal structure)
interpretations: truly human-like machines must build causal world models, ground learning in intuitive physics/psychology, and exploit compositionality plus learning-to-learn
limitations_noted: review-and-position piece rather than a new empirical or computational result
